openmeter/productcatalog/subscription/ht.purpose: v1 HTTP driver for plan-aware subscription lifecycle endpoints (create, get, list, edit, cancel, continue, restore, change, migrate, delete). Translates between api.* types and domain types, delegating to PlanSubscriptionService, SubscriptionWorkflowService, and SubscriptionService via httptransport.HandlerWithArgs.

patterns.name: HandlerWithArgs type alias pattern
patterns.description: Each operation defines four type aliases (Request, Response, Params, Handler) then implements a method on *handler returning httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs(decoder, operation, encoder, options...).

patterns.name: Namespace resolved first in every decoder
patterns.description: Every decoder lambda calls h.resolveNamespace(ctx) as its first step; failure returns a 500 via commonhttp.NewHTTPError. Namespace is never obtained from the request body.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return ..., err }

patterns.name: Custom-plan discriminator sniff via marshal+unmarshal
patterns.description: oapi-codegen union helpers (AsCustom*, AsPlan*) succeed on any structurally-serializable body, so create.go and change.go marshal the body to JSON then unmarshal into a struct checking for the customPlan field before calling As* helpers.
patterns.example: type testForCustomPlan struct { CustomPlan any `json:"customPlan"` }; json.Unmarshal(bodyBytes, &t); if t.CustomPlan != nil { body.AsCustomSubscriptionCreate() }

patterns.name: Credits guard in decoder, not in operation func
patterns.description: If !h.Credits.Enabled && req.SettlementMode == productcatalog.CreditOnlySettlementMode, the decoder returns models.NewGenericValidationError. The domain service is deployment-agnostic so the flag belongs at the HTTP boundary.

patterns.name: Centralized errorEncoder shared by all handlers
patterns.description: errors.go defines a single errorEncoder() that maps subscription.PatchConflictError, PatchForbiddenError, PatchValidationError, entitlement errors, feature.FeatureNotFoundError, and pagination.InvalidError to HTTP status codes. Every handler appends it via httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(errorEncoder()).

patterns.name: All domain-to-API conversions live in mapping.go
patterns.description: MapSubscriptionToAPI, MapSubscriptionViewToAPI, MapSubscriptionPhaseToAPI, MapSubscriptionItemToAPI, MapAPITimingToTiming, MapAPISubscriptionEditOperationToPatch, and CustomPlanToCreatePlanRequest are the only allowed conversion points.

patterns.name: HandlerConfig struct injection with all dependencies
patterns.description: Handler is implemented by *handler embedding HandlerConfig (SubscriptionWorkflowService, SubscriptionService, CustomerService, PlanSubscriptionService, NamespaceDecoder, Logger, Credits). New methods must not add dependencies outside HandlerConfig.
patterns.example: type HandlerConfig struct { SubscriptionWorkflowService subscriptionworkflow.Service; SubscriptionService subscription.Service; PlanSubscriptionService plansubscription.PlanSubscriptionService; ... }

key_file_guides.role: Defines Handler interface listing all operation methods, HandlerConfig with all dependencies, and NewHandler constructor.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Every new operation requires a new method on the Handler interface and all needed services must already be in HandlerConfig — do not inline services into individual operation files.

key_file_guides.role: All bidirectional conversions between domain and api.* types including phase item selection logic (current=active item, future=first, past=last).
key_file_guides.watch_for: MapSubscriptionPhaseToAPI's relativePhaseTime branching determines what items appear in the API response; changing this affects all GET subscription responses.

key_file_guides.role: Centralized error-to-HTTP-status mapping. ValidationIssues are handled first via commonhttp.HandleIssueIfHTTPStatusKnown, then specific domain error types via HandleErrorIfTypeMatches.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Any new domain error type that needs a non-500 HTTP status must be registered here; omitting it silently produces 500.

key_file_guides.role: CreateSubscription handler with custom-plan discriminator sniff; resolves customer by ID or key via getCustomer helper which returns GenericPreConditionFailedError (412) for deleted customers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: getCustomer must be called and its error propagated before calling PlanSubscriptionService.Create.

key_file_guides.role: ChangeSubscription handler; same custom-plan discriminator sniff as create.go; credits guard applies only in the custom plan path.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Timing is mandatory in the change body; credits guard applies only when AsCustomSubscriptionChange() path is taken.

anti_patterns: Calling SubscriptionService directly for operations that have a workflow (create, change, edit, restore, migrate) — always route through PlanSubscriptionService or SubscriptionWorkflowService.
Adding business logic to the operation func (second lambda in NewHandlerWithArgs); all validation and transformation belongs in the decoder (first lambda).
Using oapi-codegen As* helpers (body.AsCustom*, body.AsPlan*) without the marshal+unmarshal discriminator sniff — they succeed on wrong types and silently produce zero values.
Registering a new handler operation without adding its method to the Handler interface in handler.go.
Returning a domain error type not covered by errorEncoder — it silently becomes a 500.

decisions.decision: Custom-plan discriminator sniff via marshal+unmarshal instead of generated As* helpers
decisions.rationale: TypeSpec-generated union helpers succeed if the JSON is structurally serializable even for the wrong union member; field presence check is the only reliable discriminator.

decisions.decision: Credits.Enabled guard placed in the HTTP decoder, not in the domain service
decisions.rationale: The HTTP driver owns deployment configuration; domain services are deployment-agnostic so the feature flag belongs at the transport boundary.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new subscription action endpoint (e.g. PauseSubscription)

openmeter/productcatalog/subscription/se.purpose: Concrete implementation of plansubscription.PlanSubscriptionService; bridges the product catalog (plan key/version, status) to the generic subscription.Plan interface by resolving plans from the catalog, validating status, applying zeroPhasesBeforeStartingPhase, and delegating all persistence to subscriptionworkflow.Service.

patterns.name: PlanInput dual-path: ref vs inline
patterns.description: Every method accepting PlanInput first checks AsInput() for an inline custom plan (calling PlanFromPlanInput), then AsRef() for a catalog reference (calling getPlanByVersion with status validation). Both paths must end with the same WorkflowService delegation.
patterns.example: if request.PlanInput.AsInput() != nil { p, err := PlanFromPlanInput(*request.PlanInput.AsInput()) } else if request.PlanInput.AsRef() != nil { p, err := s.getPlanByVersion(ctx, ns, *request.PlanInput.AsRef()) }

patterns.name: Plan status validation before workflow delegation
patterns.description: After fetching a plan by ref, always verify p.StatusAt(clock.Now()) == productcatalog.PlanStatusActive (Migrate also allows Archived). Return models.NewGenericValidationError if not satisfied.
patterns.example: if pStatus != productcatalog.PlanStatusActive { return def, models.NewGenericValidationError(fmt.Errorf("plan %s@%d is not active at %s", p.Key, p.Version, now)) }

patterns.name: zeroPhasesBeforeStartingPhase on StartingPhase
patterns.description: When request.StartingPhase is set, always call s.zeroPhasesBeforeStartingPhase(p, *request.StartingPhase) before calling PlanFromPlan. This sets Duration to zero-duration ISODuration for phases before the named phase without removing them.
patterns.example: if request.StartingPhase != nil { if err := s.zeroPhasesBeforeStartingPhase(p, *request.StartingPhase); err != nil { return def, err } }

patterns.name: All persistence delegated to WorkflowService
patterns.description: No DB writes occur in this package; only WorkflowService.CreateFromPlan and WorkflowService.ChangeToPlan are called for actual persistence.
patterns.example: curr, new, err := s.WorkflowService.ChangeToPlan(ctx, request.ID, request.WorkflowInput, plan)

patterns.name: Migrate timing auto-selection when nil
patterns.description: When Migrate receives request.Timing == nil, it tries TimingImmediate first; if timing.ValidateForAction returns an error it falls back to TimingNextBillingCycle.
patterns.example: timing = subscription.Timing{Enum: lo.ToPtr(subscription.TimingImmediate)}; if err := timing.ValidateForAction(subscription.SubscriptionActionCancel, &currView); err != nil { timing = subscription.Timing{Enum: lo.ToPtr(subscription.TimingNextBillingCycle)} }

key_file_guides.role: Config struct (WorkflowService + SubscriptionService + PlanService + CustomerService + Logger), New() constructor returning plansubscription.PlanSubscriptionService, and zeroPhasesBeforeStartingPhase helper.
key_file_guides.watch_for: WorkflowService and SubscriptionService are intentionally separate fields; do not collapse them — they serve different roles.

key_file_guides.role: getPlanByVersion (wraps plan.Service.GetPlan, maps IsNotFound to subscription.NewPlanNotFoundError), PlanFromPlanInput (inline plan validation with temporary key='cheat'/version=1), PlanFromPlan.
key_file_guides.watch_for: PlanFromPlanInput sets Key='cheat' and Version=1 temporarily for validation; downstream code must not rely on those values being real.

key_file_guides.role: Implements Create: resolves plan, validates status, applies zeroPhasesBeforeStartingPhase, calls WorkflowService.CreateFromPlan, returns sub.Subscription (not the full view).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Returns Subscription not SubscriptionView — callers needing the view must call GetView separately.

key_file_guides.role: Implements Migrate: enforces version strictly greater than current (not equal), allows Active or Archived plan status, auto-selects timing when nil, delegates to ChangeToPlan.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Migrate allows Archived plans (unlike Change which requires Active only); the version strict-greater check is critical correctness.

anti_patterns: Adding persistence (Ent/DB) calls directly in this service — all DB writes must go through WorkflowService or SubscriptionService.
Removing or bypassing the validation cheat in PlanFromPlanInput without providing an alternative validation path for inline plans that have no real key/version yet.
Calling plan.Service.GetPlan directly from a method instead of using getPlanByVersion — the helper normalises NotFound to subscription.NewPlanNotFoundError.
Setting StartingPhase without calling zeroPhasesBeforeStartingPhase — phases before the starting phase will not collapse correctly.

decisions.decision: Plan resolution and validation are separated from WorkflowService
decisions.rationale: WorkflowService operates on the generic subscription.Plan interface and is unaware of the product catalog; PlanSubscriptionService bridges catalog concepts (key/version, status) to that interface.

decisions.decision: zeroPhasesBeforeStartingPhase sets Duration=0 instead of deleting phases
decisions.rationale: Deleting phases would change array indices and break keyed lookups; zero-duration phases are effectively skipped at the subscription layer without restructuring the phase list.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new plan-aware operation (e.g. ScheduleSubscription)

openmeter/productcatalog/subscription/te.purpose: Test-only helper package providing a PlanSubscriptionAdapter implementation that wraps plan.Service into the interface needed by service_test files, enabling plan resolution (by ref or inline input) without importing app/common Wire wiring.

patterns.name: PlanSubscriptionAdapter interface for test isolation
patterns.description: Defines a minimal PlanSubscriptionAdapter interface (GetVersion, FromInput) with a compile-time assertion (var _ PlanSubscriptionAdapter = &adapter{}) so tests can resolve plans without wiring the full DI graph.

patterns.name: GetVersion maps plan.IsNotFound to subscription.NewPlanNotFoundError
patterns.description: GetVersion delegates to plan.Service.GetPlan, maps IsNotFound errors to subscription.NewPlanNotFoundError, and checks DeletedAt before returning. Tests that bypass this mapping will see raw storage errors instead of typed domain errors.

key_file_guides.role: Entire testutils package: PlanSubscriptionAdapter interface, PlanSubscriptionAdapterConfig, adapter struct, NewPlanSubscriptionAdapter constructor, GetVersion and FromInput implementations.
key_file_guides.watch_for: GetVersion checks p.DeletedAt before returning — removing that guard silently allows deleted plans in tests. FromInput delegates to service.PlanFromPlanInput, which uses the 'cheat' key/version — tests must not rely on the returned plan having a real key or version.

anti_patterns: Importing app/common or any Wire provider from this package — it must stay independent to avoid import cycles in test dependencies.
Adding helpers that reach into the Ent DB directly instead of going through plan.Service — bypasses the same validation paths production code uses.

decisions.decision: PlanSubscriptionAdapter lives in testutils rather than being inlined in each test
decisions.rationale: Multiple service_test files need the same plan-resolution logic; the adapter DRYs that up without leaking production code paths.
